steps.title: Translate the prediction
steps.content: The hypothesis predicts that, in low visibility with large vortices, intermediate-headed fish will be more likely than narrow-headed fish to detect obstructions. If a fish detects an obstruction better, it should bump into fewer obstructions. A supporting finding therefore needs both head types in those same conditions.

steps.title: Require the direct comparison
steps.content: Choice A shows equal collision rates, which would not support a detection advantage. Choice B reports only the narrow-headed lookdown, so it lacks the needed comparison. Choice D compares specimens within the intermediate-headed species only. The supporting result must show narrow-headed fish colliding more often than intermediate-headed fish, meaning the intermediate-headed fish detected better. The answer is C.
